composite_key.description: Whether the relationship uses a composite key
composite_key.title: Composite Key

DerivationCandidate.description: Source column candidate for survivorship derivation.

table.description: Source table name (supports qualified refs: 'pipeline.table' or bare 'table'). Special value 'intermediate' indicates expression uses columns from intermediate pre-aggregation views already joined into the base context (no separate table JOIN created).
table.title: Table

column.description: Source column name (optional if expression is provided)
column.title: Column

expression.description: SQL expression for deriving value (e.g., 'CONCAT_WS(" ", fname, lname)'). Use instead of column for computed values.
expression.title: Expression

reason.description: Explanation of why this source was selected and its priority
reason.title: Reason

join_filter.description: SQL WHERE clause for filtering this table join (e.g., 'pcp_type = "P4Q"'). Used when joining same table multiple times with different filters.
join_filter.title: Join Filter

table_instance.description: Unique alias for this table+filter combination (e.g., 'pcp_assigned', 'pcp_imputed'). Required when joining same table multiple times with different filters to disambiguate joins.
table_instance.title: Table Instance

join_via.description: Specification for joining through an intermediary lookup table. Used when the source table's key doesn't directly match the target's primary key.

order_by.description: Columns for window function ORDER BY (all DESC). When specified, uses ROW_NUMBER() window function instead of GROUP BY aggregation to get the full row with max value. E.g., ['result_approval_date', 'meta_snapshot_dt'] for max row by date then file timestamp.
order_by.title: Order By

row_filter.description: SQL WHERE clause to filter source rows before aggregation. E.g., 'test_name = "Glyco HGB A1C" AND result IS NOT NULL'. Used with window functions to pre-filter qualifying records.
row_filter.title: Row Filter

select_columns.description: Additional columns to include in the aggregation view output. Used with window functions to preserve traceability columns like result value, meta_source_name, loinc code, etc. E.g., ['result', 'meta_source_name', 'meta_snapshot_dt'].
select_columns.title: Select Columns

DerivationCandidate.title: DerivationCandidate

FileFormatSpec.description: File format specification for data ingestion.

skip_rows.description: Number of rows to skip at start
skip_rows.title: Skip Rows

filename_pattern.description: Pattern for extracting metadata from filenames into table columns

source_directory.description: Subdirectory name to look for files in (when different from table name). Used when files for multiple tables are stored in the same folder.
source_directory.title: Source Directory

FileFormatSpec.title: FileFormatSpec

FilenamePattern.description: Filename pattern for extracting metadata from filenames.

captures.description: Mapping from capture group index to column name (e.g., {1: 'source_vendor', 7: 'mode'})
captures.title: Captures

FilenamePattern.title: FilenamePattern

cross_pipeline.description: Whether this FK references a table in a different pipeline
cross_pipeline.title: Cross Pipeline

IncomingRelationship.description: Incoming foreign key relationship from another table.

source_table.description: Table that references this table (supports qualified refs: 'pipeline.table' or bare 'table')
source_table.title: Source Table

source_column.description: Column in source table that references this table
source_column.title: Source Column

target_column.description: Column in this table being referenced
target_column.title: Target Column

IncomingRelationship.title: IncomingRelationship

IngestionConfig.description:
Configuration for ingestion behavior.

Controls how data from multiple files is deduplicated and written.

mode.description: 'snapshot': Filter to latest file, overwrite table. 'incremental': Keep latest per PK, upsert to table.
mode.title: Mode

order_by.description: Columns to determine 'latest' (sorted descending). Can include: filename-extracted columns (e.g., 'file_date_yyyymmdd') or metadata columns (e.g., 'meta_source_name', 'meta_snapshot_dt', 'meta_load_dt'). For snapshot mode: filters to rows with MAX values of these columns. For incremental mode: orders rows when deduping by primary key.
order_by.title: Order By

pre_upsert_exclusions.description: Cross-pipeline exclusion rules applied before upsert. Matching rows are dropped, preserving existing ingested data for those keys.
pre_upsert_exclusions.title: Pre Upsert Exclusions

post_upsert_rules.description: Rules applied after upsert to set flags (e.g., soft-delete) on ingested data.
post_upsert_rules.title: Post Upsert Rules

IngestionConfig.title: IngestionConfig

IngestionExclusionRule.description:
Pre-upsert exclusion rule using cross-table data.

Rows matching the exclusion are dropped before upsert, preserving
existing ingested data for those keys.

join_column.description: Column in this table to join on
join_column.title: Join Column

cross_pipeline_join_column.description: Column in the cross-pipeline table to join on
cross_pipeline_join_column.title: Cross Pipeline Join Column

exclude_when.description: SQL expression evaluated on joined row. When true, the row is excluded from upsert. References this table's columns as 'src.*' and cross-pipeline columns as 'xref.*'.
exclude_when.title: Exclude When

IngestionExclusionRule.title: IngestionExclusionRule

JoinViaSpec.description:
Specification for joining through an intermediary lookup table.

Used when the source table's join key doesn't directly match the target's primary key.
For example, when a truncated key needs to be resolved through an intermediary
table to get the full identifier.

source_key.description: Key column in the base table (e.g., 'client_member_id')
source_key.title: Source Key

lookup_key.description: Key column in lookup table that matches source_key (e.g., 'member_id')
lookup_key.title: Lookup Key

target_key.description: Key column that links lookup table to source table (e.g., 'insurance_policy')
target_key.title: Target Key

JoinViaSpec.title: JoinViaSpec

Nullable.description: Nullable configuration per Line of Business.
Nullable.title: Nullable

OutgoingRelationship.description: Outgoing foreign key relationship to another table.

target_table.description: Table that this table references (supports qualified refs: 'pipeline.table' or bare 'table')
target_table.title: Target Table

source_column.description: Column in this table that references target table
source_column.title: Source Column

target_column.description: Column in target table being referenced
target_column.title: Target Column

OutgoingRelationship.title: OutgoingRelationship

include_footer.description: Include row count footer in output files
include_footer.title: Include Footer

PostUpsertRule.description:
Post-upsert rule to update flags on ingested data.

Supports optional cross-pipeline table references for conditions that need
to check external data (e.g., mark as deleted if member disenrolled in another table).

condition.description: SQL condition identifying rows to flag. Can reference table columns directly. Use '{this_table}' as placeholder for the current table name. If cross_pipeline_table is set, use '{xref}' to reference the cross-pipeline table.
condition.title: Condition

cross_pipeline_table.description: Optional cross-pipeline table ref: 'pipeline.table' for conditions that need external data. When set, the table is registered as '{xref}' temp view.
cross_pipeline_table.title: Cross Pipeline Table

join_column.description: Column in this table to join on (required if cross_pipeline_table is set)
join_column.title: Join Column

cross_pipeline_join_column.description: Column in the cross-pipeline table to join on (required if cross_pipeline_table is set)
cross_pipeline_join_column.title: Cross Pipeline Join Column

PostUpsertRule.title: PostUpsertRule

QualityCheck.description:
Single data quality check executed on typed/ingested data.

Quality checks run on typed data AFTER type conversion.
They check data values, business rules, and cross-column constraints.

See INGESTED_QUALITY_CHECK_TYPES for allowed expectation types.

expectation.description: Great Expectations expectation configuration (type + kwargs + meta)
expectation.title: Expectation

severity.description: Severity level: critical (halt), error (fail), warning (log), info (report)
severity.title: Severity

blocking.description: If True, pipeline execution halts when this check fails. Default: False (non-blocking)
blocking.title: Blocking

description.description: Human-readable description of what this check validates
description.title: Description

QualityCheck.title: QualityCheck

QualityChecks.description:
Data quality checks executed on typed/ingested data.

These checks run on typed data AFTER type conversion.
They validate data values, business rules, and relationships.

See INGESTED_QUALITY_CHECK_TYPES for allowed check types.

For schema/structure checks on raw strings, use validation_rules instead.

checks.description: List of quality checks to execute on ingested data
checks.title: Checks

QualityChecks.title: QualityChecks

hub_score.description: Hub score indicating centrality in relationship graph
hub_score.title: Hub Score

outgoing.description: Outgoing relationships from this table to other tables
outgoing.title: Outgoing

incoming.description: Incoming relationships from tables that reference this table
incoming.title: Incoming

Survivorship.description: Survivorship strategy for multi-source column derivation.

explanation.description: Comprehensive explanation of mapping strategy, source selection/rejection reasoning, candidates examined, business rules applied, and fallback behavior
explanation.title: Explanation

default_value.description: Default value to use when no source data is available or all candidates are null. Extracted from target column description (e.g., 'default to 0', 'Default it to IHA').
default_value.title: Default Value

default_condition.description: Condition or context for when the default value should be applied. Extracted from target column description (e.g., 'until assessment is completed', 'when not available').
default_condition.title: Default Condition

Survivorship.title: Survivorship

canonical_name.description: Canonical column name from source specifications (original case, any format). Used for input/output file headers. Can contain spaces, special characters, etc. If not provided, defaults to name.
canonical_name.title: Canonical Name

aliases.description: Alternative names for this column (e.g., variations in spelling, case, or abbreviations). Used for resolving column name mismatches when mapping between different data sources.
aliases.title: Aliases

format.description: Unstructured format pattern or example from source specification. May contain date patterns (YYYY-MM-DD), value enumerations (M, F, U), structural patterns (State_LOB), or example values ('40', '1.85'). This field preserves vendor documentation as-is and requires context-aware interpretation based on the data_type and column purpose.
format.title: Format

fallback_formats.description: Alternative date/timestamp formats to try if primary format fails. Used when source data has mixed format conventions (e.g., M/D/YYYY and MM-DD-YYYY). Formats are tried in order after the primary format fails.
fallback_formats.title: Fallback Formats

notes.description: Additional notes or business rules from source specification. Contains unstructured documentation that provides context for the column.
notes.title: Notes

source.description: Source of column value: 'data' (from file content), 'filename' (parsed from filename), 'metadata' (from ingestion metadata), or 'derived' (computed at runtime in Silver/Gold layers)
source.title: Source

key_type.description: Key type for constraint-aware generation: 'primary', 'unique', 'foreign_one_to_one', or 'foreign_one_to_many'
key_type.title: Key Type

exclude_from_change_detection.description: When True, exclude this column from change detection hash. Use for columns whose values change every run (e.g., rundate) but should not trigger a record to be treated as 'changed'.
exclude_from_change_detection.title: Exclude From Change Detection

derived_from.description: DEPRECATED: Use 'derivation' field instead. Source column name when this column is derived from another column (e.g., PBPType derived from source_lob)
derived_from.title: Derived From

derivation_mapping.description: DEPRECATED: Use 'derivation' field instead. Value mapping for derivation: {source_value: derived_value} (e.g., {'MD': 'MEDICAID', 'ME': 'MEDICARE'})
derivation_mapping.title: Derivation Mapping

derivation_expression.description: DEPRECATED: Use 'derivation' field instead. Python expression for complex derivations.
derivation_expression.title: Derivation Expression

domain_type.description: Domain type classification (e.g., 'us_state_code', 'email', 'phone_number', 'member_id'). Used to apply standard validation rules, sample data generators, and format patterns. Inferred during spec generation based on column name and metadata.
domain_type.title: Domain Type

derivation.description: Multi-source derivation metadata with survivorship strategy. Specifies candidate source columns and logic for selecting the best value. Used for generated tables that consolidate data from multiple sources.

provenance_policy.description: Data provenance policy for survivorship mapping: 'enterprise_only' (never use outreach files), 'enterprise_preferred' (prefer enterprise; use outreach as fallback), 'outreach_only' (only use outreach files), 'survivorship' (standard multi-source logic)
provenance_policy.title: Provenance Policy

provenance_notes.description: Human-readable explanation of provenance policy and data source expectations
provenance_notes.title: Provenance Notes

pivot_field.description: Whether this field is part of a numbered pivot sequence (e.g., GAP1, GAP2, GAP3)
pivot_field.title: Pivot Field

pivot_source_table.description: Source table for pivot field (e.g., 'outreach_list_gaps' for GAP1, GAP2, etc.)
pivot_source_table.title: Pivot Source Table

pivot_source_column.description: Source column name to pivot from (e.g., 'quality_gap_group' for GAP fields)
pivot_source_column.title: Pivot Source Column

pivot_index.description: Position in the pivot sequence (e.g., 1 for GAP1, 2 for GAP2, etc.)
pivot_index.title: Pivot Index

pivot_max_count.description: Maximum number of pivot fields in the sequence (e.g., 6 for GAP1-GAP6)
pivot_max_count.title: Pivot Max Count

default.description: Default value to use for this column when no data is provided or value is null. Commonly used in disposition tracking and status fields.
default.title: Default

profiling.description: Profiling metadata for data quality expectations (e.g., completeness, approximate_num_distinct). Used to generate profiling-based Great Expectations constraints.
profiling.title: Profiling

null_output_value.description: String value to use when rendering NULL values in output files. When specified, NULL values are replaced with this string during output formatting. When not specified, NULL values are rendered according to the CSV writer's nullValue option (empty string by default). Example: 'NA', 'N/A', 'NULL', '9999-12-31'
null_output_value.title: Null Output Value

preserve_literal_null.description: If True, the string 'NULL' (case-insensitive) is preserved as a literal string value during ingestion. If False (default), 'NULL' strings are converted to SQL NULL. Use this for columns where 'NULL' is a valid data value (e.g., someone's actual last name is 'NULL').
preserve_literal_null.title: Preserve Literal Null

UMFColumnDerivation.description:
Column derivation metadata for multi-source survivorship.

Used when a column in a generated table is derived from multiple source tables,
requiring survivorship logic to choose the best value.

Note: Either candidates, survivorship, or strategy must be present.
Enterprise-only fields may have survivorship metadata without candidates.
Primary key columns use strategy: primary_key without candidates.

strategy.description: Derivation strategy for special column types. Use 'primary_key' for columns that should be derived from the base table's primary key column, or 'base_column' for columns that come directly from the base table.
strategy.title: Strategy

explanation.description: Human-readable explanation of the derivation logic for documentation.
explanation.title: Explanation

candidates.description: Source columns this field can be derived from, ordered by priority. May be omitted for enterprise-only fields that have no source candidates.
candidates.title: Candidates

survivorship.description: Survivorship strategy for selecting among candidates

UMFColumnDerivation.title: UMFColumnDerivation

pipeline_phase.description: Pipeline phase that created/updated this file
pipeline_phase.title: Pipeline Phase

source_file_modified.description: Last modified timestamp of the source Excel file
source_file_modified.title: Source File Modified

base_table_strategy.description: Strategy for building base table. Use 'union_sources' to build from UNION of source_tables.
base_table_strategy.title: Base Table Strategy

base_table.description: Explicit base table name for SQL generation. Overrides automatic base table inference.
base_table.title: Base Table

source_tables.description: Source table names for union_sources strategy.
source_tables.title: Source Tables

unpivot_columns.description: Source columns to UNPIVOT into rows.
unpivot_columns.title: Unpivot Columns

unpivot_value_column.description: Output column name for the unpivoted values.
unpivot_value_column.title: Unpivot Value Column

dedup_strategy.description: Deduplication strategy for generated tables. 'latest': deduplicate on primary_key, keeping the row with the most recent load date.
dedup_strategy.title: Dedup Strategy

example.source_file: Centene Outbound Outreach Data Layouts v2.5 2026.xlsx

canonical_name.description: Canonical name from source specifications (original case, any format). Can contain spaces, special characters, etc. Exact match to Excel tab name or specification.
canonical_name.title: Canonical Name

aliases.description: Alternative names for this table (e.g., sheet name variations, case variants). Used for resolving references when table names don't match exactly.
aliases.title: Aliases

source_sheet_name.description: Original Excel sheet name from source specification. Stored for provenance tracking when canonical_name is derived from filename patterns.
source_sheet_name.title: Source Sheet Name

table_type.description: Table classification: data_table, lookup_table, or configuration
table_type.title: Table Type

primary_key.description: Primary key column names (single or compound).
primary_key.title: Primary Key

unique_constraints.description: Unique constraint column combinations (non-primary compound uniqueness). Each inner list represents a set of columns that must be unique together.
unique_constraints.title: Unique Constraints

file_format.description: File format specification for ingestion (delimiter, encoding, etc.)

quality_checks.description: Post-ingestion data quality checks for fitness assessment

lookup_metadata.description: Lookup table metadata including sample data and structure
lookup_metadata.title: Lookup Metadata

sample_data_cases.description: Forced test cases for sample data generation. Each dict defines column values that must be included in generated sample data to test specific scenarios (e.g., edge cases, cross-table relationships).
sample_data_cases.title: Sample Data Cases

derivations.description: Derivation metadata including LLM generation info, mappings, survivorship strategies, and normalization rules
derivations.title: Derivations

ingestion.description: Ingestion strategy. Controls file filtering, deduplication, and write mode.
